creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #16)

My notes do not explain why varying total_mem= values happens
to lead to working vs. non-working boots, depending on the
value.

It seems to be related to variability in "Physical memory chunk(s)".
For example:

0x00000040000000 - 0x0000007163bfff, 828620800 bytes (202300 pages)
for:
       BootServicesData 000040000000 000040000000 00034200 WB

vs.

0x00000040000000 - 0x00000071542fff, 827600896 bytes (202051 pages)
for:
       BootServicesData 000040000000 000040000000 00034100 WB
